sourceMovie.title: Jules
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Jules (2023) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Cocoon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jules clicked for you, Cocoon (1985) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and science fiction, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jules?
faq.answer: Try Cocoon, Robot & Frank, Arrival—each shares DNA with Jules while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jules?
faq.answer: Jules (2023) is best described as Comedy, Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jules worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
